title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. viscidiflorus
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. viscidiflorus, also known as greasewood, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of greasewood include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Greasewood is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
